title: Racosperma ptychocladum
taxonomy: Racosperma ptychocladum is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia and is part of the genus Racosperma. The plant was previously classified as Acacia ptychocladum but was reclassified as Racosperma ptychocladum in some classifications. Racosperma ptychocladum is a shrub or tree that grows in a variety of environments, from tropical to temperate regions. The species has been the subject of taxonomic debate, with some sources continuing to recognize it as Acacia ptychocladum.
